name: Pork Chops Abazia
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground white pepper
6 large Very ripe tomatoes, seeded, peeled, coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Dry the pork chops with paper towels.
2. Season with salt and pepper.
3. In a large heavy skillet, heat 3 T olive oil until it is almost smoking.
4. Brown the chops for 2 or 3 minutes on each side and remove them to a plate.
5. Pour out all but 2 T of the fat.
6. Lower the heat.
7. Add the shallots and garlic and cook until soft, making sure not to burn them.
8. Add the paprika, tomatoes, and wine. Bring the mixture to a boil and lower the heat.
9. Add the hot chili pepper, tomato paste, green pepper, and rosemary.
10. Return the chops to the pan. Cover tightly and let simmer for 45 minutes.
11. Five minutes before they are ready, add the roasted red peppers.
12. Remove the chops to a serving dish.
13. If the sauce seems a little thin, raise the heat and reduce it until it heavily coats the spoon.
14. Discard the rosemary.
15. Correct the seasoning and pour the sauce over the chops.
16. Garnish with parsley and serve immediately.
Note: This dish can be cooked in a 350 degree oven for 45 to 50 minutes and can be served over rice pilaf.
